skill.name: create-node
skill.description: Guides developers through creating custom FlowGram nodes for the demo-free-layout app, including simple schema-driven nodes and complex custom UI nodes.
skill.summary: Create FlowGram custom nodes with reusable templates and clear registration steps.
skill.author: bytedance

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, crypto, and heuristic issues, but context shows they are documentation snippets, placeholder templates, relative imports, and reference links. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious execution intent was found. The remaining risk is low because users may copy local shell commands that modify project files.

low_findings.title: Documentation Shell Commands Require User Care
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for local file modification guidance, but not malicious. The cp, rush, find, and sed examples are user-run documentation snippets for creating FlowGram node files. The optional replacement command can alter project files if used with the wrong path or untrusted values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly inside instructional Markdown and require explicit user execution. Risk remains because the examples copy templates and run search-and-replace across project files.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Findings Are Relative Imports
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The detected ../ sequences are TypeScript relative imports to nearby FlowGram components, hooks, typings, assets, and constants. No user-controlled path joins, file reads, or arbitrary filesystem traversal were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every reviewed path traversal hit is an import path or documentation import example. There is no dynamic path construction or filesystem API usage.

low_findings.title: Network URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs are a placeholder input value and public documentation links for FlowGram, JSON Schema, and Semi UI. No code performs HTTP requests or transmits data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are examples or reference links in Markdown. No fetch, request library, webhook, or network execution path appears in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Crypto, Credential, and Obfuscation Alerts Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. Static alerts for weak cryptography, certificate/key material, reconnaissance, and dangerous combinations appear to match ordinary terms such as node IDs, key field names, placeholder code, and high-entropy documentation text. No secrets, encoded payloads, prompt injection, or malicious control flow were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found normal frontmatter, nanoid usage, UI field names, and template metadata. The scanner signals are broad heuristics and are not supported by executable malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: FlowGram developers need repeatable steps for adding custom workflow nodes. This skill provides templates and guidance for schema-driven nodes, custom UI nodes, registration, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Explains the FlowGram node data model with inputsValues, inputs, and outputs.
Guides creation of simple nodes that use schema-generated forms.
Guides creation of complex nodes with custom React form components.
Provides template files for node registry, form metadata, types, and components.
Lists registration steps for constants, node exports, icons, and local testing.
limitations: Assumes the FlowGram demo-free-layout project structure is present.
Requires developers to replace placeholders before using the templates.
Does not implement backend execution logic for new node behavior.
Does not automatically validate generated code or run project tests.

use_cases.title: Add a Basic Configuration Node
use_cases.description: Create a node whose form can be generated from JSON Schema fields and defaults.

use_cases.title: Build a Custom Node Editor
use_cases.description: Create a node that needs custom React controls, layout, and output display.

use_cases.title: Standardize Node Contributions
use_cases.description: Give contributors a consistent checklist for templates, registration, icons, and testing.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Node Type
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me decide whether my new FlowGram node should use the simple template or the complex template. My node needs: [describe fields and UI behavior].

prompt_templates.title: Plan Node Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the inputsValues, inputs, and outputs for a FlowGram node that does this: [describe behavior]. Keep the form simple when possible.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Template-Based Node
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through creating a new FlowGram node named [name] from the [simple or complex] template. Include files to copy and placeholders to replace.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Custom Node
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my FlowGram custom node design for schema consistency, registration steps, read-only handling, output fields, and test readiness.

output_examples.input: Create a simple node for collecting an API URL, timeout, and prompt.
output: Field plan with required URL, numeric timeout, and template prompt.
Registration checklist for constants, exports, icon, and local validation.

output_examples.input: Create a complex node that needs a custom selector and output preview.
output: Template file plan for registry, form metadata, types, and custom component.
Review notes for read-only state and output display.

output_examples.input: Review whether my node data fields are consistent.
output: Consistency findings for initial values, form schema, and outputs.
Testing steps for the demo-free-layout app.

best_practices: Keep each node focused on one clear workflow responsibility.
Define inputs, initial values, and outputs together so saved data stays consistent.
Test the node in the demo app after registration and placeholder replacement.
anti_patterns: Do not leave template placeholders in committed node files.
Do not use a complex custom UI when schema-generated fields are enough.
Do not define form values that are missing from the input schema.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create custom FlowGram workflow nodes for the demo-free-layout application.

faq.question: When should I use the simple node template?
faq.answer: Use it when the node can be configured through standard schema-generated form fields.

faq.question: When should I use the complex node template?
faq.answer: Use it when the node needs custom layout, special controls, or custom React interactions.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands and templates that a developer can choose to run locally.

faq.question: Does it include backend node execution code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on FlowGram node configuration, UI templates, registration, and validation steps.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
